summary: One Jest test suite (containing 1 test) failed out of 65 total suites, causing the pnpm recursive test run to exit with code 1. The specific failing test assertion is not shown in the log, but the overall Jest run reported 1 failure out of 485 tests, which propagated as [ERR_PNPM_RECURSIVE_RUN_FIRST_FAIL].
root_cause: One Jest test failed (1 of 485 tests across 1 of 65 suites), causing the jest process to exit with code 1 and pnpm to abort the recursive test run with ERR_PNPM_RECURSIVE_RUN_FIRST_FAIL.

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Jest explicitly reports that 1 test suite failed, which is the direct cause of the non-zero exit.
Confirms exactly 1 test failed at the assertion level, not a configuration or compile issue.
pnpm stopped the recursive run because the jest command exited non-zero, confirming the failure originated in jest.
Exit code 1 from the jest invocation propagated up to terminate the CI job.
A potential runtime error mid-run that may be related to the failing test, though the specific test name is not shown in the log.

suggested_fix: Identify the failing test suite by re-running jest with --verbose or inspecting the full jest output for the FAIL line; once the specific test is known, reproduce locally and fix the assertion or underlying behavior it exercises.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
